items.title: Frontier Airlines to debut in-flight Wi-Fi in 2027 with SpaceX's Starlink
items.description: Frontier is signing with Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi, with service debuting as early as next year.

items.title: U.S. oil tops $80 after Trump bombs Iran for third day, Tehran attacks tankers in Hormuz
items.description: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to impose shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Global shipping industry sounds the alarm over Trump’s Hormuz toll plan
items.description: For shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd, it is "fundamentally wrong" to charge tolls for passage through international waters, regardless of the country in charge.

items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
items.description: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

items.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
items.description: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

items.title: Warsh promises inflation will be a 'thing of the past,' cites benefits of AI investment boom
items.description: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

items.title: Consumers turn to buy now, pay later for essential expenses — with growing risks
items.description: Some consumers are using buy now, pay later for essential expenses like groceries, rent and utility bills. But more BNPL users have recently had a late payment.

items.title: TikTok policy chief defends safety measures amid EU push to limit children's social media access
items.description: TikTok's policy chief defended the platform's safety measures as the European Union pushes ahead with restricting children's access to social media.

items.title: IBM shares skid more than 20% after company warns second-quarter earnings fell short of expectations
items.description: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

items.title: World Cup's biggest spenders show up late as semifinals drive host city travel boom
items.description: World Cup travel demand to U.S. host cities is spiking as the tournament reaches the semifinal stage.

items.title: Retail-trader 'bottleneck bros' eyeing AI supply chain can't wait for SK Hynix options
items.description: For many of those retail tech traders, SK Hynix's memory business reminds them of Micron – the paragon of the supply-chain bottleneck thesis.

items.title: A French underwear brand is taking on fast fashion — with an IPO
items.description: Le Slip Français will make its stock market debut in Paris on Tuesday, betting that consumers will pay for locally made clothing amid competition from Shein.

items.title: Business declares war on Pentagon stock buyback, dividend restrictions moving in Senate
items.description: The provision was pushed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., and was included in the National Defense Authorization Act on a bipartisan basis.

items.title: Is Hormuz open? Trump's toll threat intensifies rush to bypass the Strait altogether
items.description: Gulf producers are increasingly relying on alternative routes to keep crude moving as shipping disruptions expose the risks of depending on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: U.S. targets Iranian military assets in another round of strikes as Tehran hits Gulf states
items.description: The U.S. has completed a third consecutive night of strikes against Iran, as Tehran targets U.S. military facilities in the region.

items.title: China exports in June rise at fastest pace since 2021 as AI boom, tariff rush lift trade
items.description: China's exports to the U.S. jumped around 14% last month while imports grew 26%, according to CNBC calculation of the official data.

items.title: Taiwan's second-largest chipmaker starts mass production in Singapore; Citi sees improving outlook
items.description: Taiwan’s UMC has kicked off mass production of silicon photonics wafers at its Singapore facility amid positive Citi forecast.

items.title: 'Nobody underwrote for that': Private credit faces a key test as higher rates squeeze borrowers
items.description: Private credit is facing a new pressure point from elevated rates, which could hit under-pressure borrowers.

items.title: Trump's Hormuz toll plans bring oil supply risks back in spotlight
items.description: While the proposed levy would raise shipping costs, an escalating conflict could remove barrels from the market altogether.

items.title: 'Listing is a must': Chinese humanoid startups are rushing to launch IPOs
items.description: Shenzhen-based LimX Dynamics is the latest humanoid company in China to raise capital in recent weeks as investors eye IPO exits.
